overview.description: Complete guide to creating a Dungeons & Dragons character using building blocks of class, background, species, and personal details
overview.components: Game statistics, roleplaying hooks, and imagination
overview.avatar_purpose: Your character serves as your avatar in the D&D multiverse

talk_with_dm.importance: Essential first step before character creation
discussion_topics: Type of D&D game the DM plans to run
Character concepts that fit the game

examples.greek_mythology: Character choices differ for mythological campaigns
examples.swashbuckling: High seas adventures suggest different character types
examples.horror: Gothic campaigns may favor certain themes

talk_with_dm.key_question: What kind of adventurer do you want to play in this game?

session_zero.definition: Initial gathering focused on creating characters and setting expectations
opportunities: Talk with other players and DM
Decide if characters know each other
session_zero.benefits: Ensures character concepts work together and fit the campaign

character_sheet_selection.definition: Choose the format for recording character details
character_sheet_selection.recommendation: Choose whichever style works best for you
character_sheet_selection.flexibility: The term 'character sheet' encompasses any method of tracking character details

step_1.title: Choose a Class
step_1.description: Every adventurer is a member of a class
step_1.class_definition: Broadly describes a character's vocation, special talents, and favored tactics
considerations: Combat role preference (tank, damage, support, control)

examples.fighter: Martial combat specialist with weapon mastery
examples.wizard: Arcane spellcaster with vast magical knowledge
examples.cleric: Divine magic user with healing and support

step_1.reference: See classes/ directory for complete class options and details

step_2.title: Determine Origin
step_2.description: A character's origin includes two elements: background and species

background.question: How did the character spend the years leading up to a life of adventure?

languages.importance: Enable communication and understanding of written materials

step_2.integration: Background and species work together to create character history and capabilities
step_2.reference: See backgrounds/ and species/ directories for complete options

step_3.title: Determine Ability Scores
step_3.description: Much of what your character does in the game depends on the character's six abilities
step_3.importance: Ability scores affect nearly every aspect of character performance
considerations: Class primary abilities (most important for class features)
Secondary abilities (useful for skills and saves)
Racial bonuses and how they interact with class needs
step_3.reference: See rules/abilities.json for complete ability score system

step_4.title: Choose an Alignment
step_4.description: Alignment is a shorthand for your character's moral compass
step_4.purpose: Provides guidance for character behavior and decision-making
step_4.flexibility: Alignment can evolve based on character experiences and growth

step_5.title: Fill in Details
step_5.description: Using the choices you've made, fill in the remaining details on your character sheet

mechanical_details: Spell save DC and spell attack bonus (if applicable)

tips: Leave room for character growth and development
Consider how details connect to other party members
Think about plot hooks for the DM to use
Make the character engaging to roleplay

character_concept_development.integration_approach: Weave class, background, species, and personality into a cohesive whole
questions_to_consider: What drove this character to become an adventurer?
How do their background experiences shape their worldview?
What are their goals, both personal and professional?
How do they interact with authority, strangers, and friends?
What internal or external conflicts drive their story?
How does their species heritage influence their perspective?
What role do they prefer in a group dynamic?

fallen_paladin.hook: Lost faith after witnessing corruption, seeking redemption
fallen_paladin.personality: Struggling with doubt but maintaining hope

street_smart_wizard.hook: Self-taught magic to survive, now pursuing formal education
street_smart_wizard.personality: Clever and resourceful with trust issues

noble_ranger.hook: Left comfort to protect wilderness from family's industrial expansion
noble_ranger.personality: Conflicted between duty to family and nature

setting_connections: Ties to locations, organizations, or NPCs
Knowledge of local customs and politics
Reasons to care about the central conflict
plot_hooks: Personal goals that align with campaign
Enemies or rivals from character's past

starting_statistics.hit_points: Maximum at 1st level (class HD + Constitution modifier)
starting_statistics.armor_class: 10 + Dex modifier + armor bonus + shield bonus

proficiencies.skills: From class, background, and sometimes species

equipment.considerations: Armor Class, weapon proficiencies, adventure utility

spellcasting.if_applicable: Calculate spell save DC and spell attack bonus

finalization_checklist: All ability scores assigned and bonuses calculated
Hit points, AC, and other defenses calculated
Connections to party and campaign identified

references.classes: See classes/ directory for all class options and features
references.backgrounds: See backgrounds/ directory for all background options
references.species: See species/ directory for all species options and traits
references.equipment: See equipment/ directory for weapons, armor, and gear
references.spells: See spells/ directory for all spell options
references.rules: See rules/ directory for game mechanics and systems
references.abilities: See rules/abilities.json for ability score details
references.alignment: See rules for alignment descriptions and implications
